This is the 44th launch from OpenAI. View more

## GPT-5.6

Launching today

New standard for both intelligence and efficiency [...] New standard for both intelligence and efficiency

GPT-5.6 is rolling out in the API! Our new family of models gives builders three options: Sol (our flagship model for the hardest agentic tasks), Terra (balanced for everyday workflows), and Luna (fast and cost-efficient). GPT-5.6 also introduces Programmatic Tool Calling, Multi-agent (beta) for parallel execution, and explicit prompt caching. On July 23, we're teaming up with Product Hunt for OpenAI Day. The top five launches will each receive $10K in API credits.

results.title: New: GPT-5.6 makes Toyo better at complex work : Toyo Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
This is a meaningful upgrade that gives Toyo greater intelligence across the board. Notably, it's not just smarter, but more reliable at complex tool calling. In practice, this just means Toyo is better at keeping hold of all of the details of a task as it moves between the tools you already use.

For example: Let's say an email turns into a meeting request. Toyo needs to understand the thread, check your calendar, use context from another integration and draft the reply. Real work rarely fits inside a single chat response.

## No setup, no settings, no model picker [...] ### Topic Forums

# New: GPT-5.6 makes Toyo better at complex work

Toyo

Toyo

This morning, OpenAI released their new GPT-5.6 models. We sprinted to add support in Toyo, and it's now live! [...] CHRISTIAN ONOCHIE

The email-to-meeting example really clicked for me. Most real workflows jump between multiple apps, so handling that context smoothly is a huge improvement.

Debra Salt

I appreciate the philosophy here. Instead of asking users to learn another set of model names, you're keeping the experience simple and letting the product do the heavy lifting.

Greffin Dony

This feels like the right direction. People don't really care which model is running under the hood they just want their assistant to complete complex tasks accurately and consistently.

results.title: OpenAI Discussion Forums
results.content:
Report

Mustafa Najoom

Bigger model, easier demo, harder production, not the other way around. GPT-5.6 will nail the flashy first pass, but you still have to decide what happens when it's confidently wrong and who approves what it actually does.

Report

Gal Dayan

worth flagging for anyone scheduling around this: every launch competing for the same $10k pool is also landing on Product Hunt the same day, so you're not just racing to build something good with GPT-5.6, you're racing every other GPT-5.6 launch for the same day's attention. might be worth staggering a day or two off July 23 if the goal is actually getting seen rather than qualifying for the prize

Report

# Product Hunt 🤝 OpenAI

by

Product Hunt

Product Hunt

•

OpenAI just dropped GPT-5.6, their largest model yet, and we're teaming up with them to get it into makers' hands.

Here's the deal:  
  
- Build something with Sol, Terra, or Luna  
- Schedule your launch on Product Hunt for July 23

- The top products walk away with $10k in OpenAI credits

If you've had an idea sitting in your notes app waiting for a model that could actually pull it off, this is the excuse. GPT-5.6 launched here just a few days ago and the early projects coming out of it are already impressive.

Drop what you're planning to build below and remember to schedule your launch 👀

Erik Aronesty

I will be scheduling launch of prismclip.com on jul 23d, It's a in beta now, but the feedback system is all automated using sol. It looks at the user's creations, finds problematic edits and corrects the algorithm in a loop. It also reads user's feedback, prints linear tickets, and another agent picks them up, handles them, and another agent pushes them to staging. Sol is crazy good.

results.title: OpenAI wants your launches
results.content:
newsletter icon

July 14th, 2026

# OpenAI wants your launches

## OpenAI wants your launches

Today: an AI agent gets its own debit card, a guard dog moves in with your database, and your trackpad learns to breathe. Plus $10k in OpenAI credits for whatever you build next.

## We teamed up with OpenAI

OpenAI just dropped GPT-5.6, their largest model yet, and it's the first model in a while that has a lot of us opening a different terminal: trying a new harness, moving workflows over, comparing outputs. The labs leapfrogging each other is how everyone building on top ends up with better tools.
